Dear Colleagues,

 

The Data Intelligence team is pleased to share the release of a series of important updates to our AI models, the result of focused work and collaboration over the summer. These developments bring meaningful improvements to our forecasting capabilities and aim to support better performance throughout our OPTI operations. Additionally, we are increasingly integrating data products with our AI models to capitalize on the strength of this new data governance approach.

 

Highlights of the New Release

Solar Forecast (SPP)
In July, we launched next-generation models that deliver a 2-3% improvement in MAPE, boosting performance for both DAH and ID operations.
If you need more details, feel free to ask @Mériot, Clément
RLP Demand Forecast
Last Thursday, we released a state-of-the-art ExtraTrees model leveraging cutting-edge weather data from Meteomatics.
We anticipate a 0.7% reduction in MAPE—an huge gain taking into account the size of this portfolio.
Experiments with deep learning (CVSN) are showing promise for probabilistic forecasts (aka uncertainty indicators) , opening the door to even greater operational confidence (in the limit of the accuracy of the weather forecasts). Stay tuned! 
If you need more details, feel free to ask @Ternot, Benjamin, @Begon, Jean-Michel & @Mormont, Romain
Wind Offshore Forecast
Our oldest forecast project receives a full-scale upgrade, replacing legacy solutions (XGBoost, power curve, neural networks) with a powerful suite: ExtraTrees, fine-tuned power curves, and deep learning (CVSN).
Furthermore, we are utilizing the data product for Elia metering data, building upon the work done by our DNA colleagues with the metering hub
We observed several percent MAPE improvement compared to the baseline during experimentation. While the performance margin can vary in production, we expect a significant MAPE improvement (DAH and ID)
If you need more details, feel free to ask @Mormont, Romain & @Begon, Jean-Michel
Wind Onshore
New wind parks added
Preparation work for THE bigger release of this autumn/winter
If you need more details, feel free to ask @Begon, Jean-Michel & @Mormont, Romain
CCGT Weather Forecast
Our upgraded model now seamlessly integrates with the ACCP project and follows a data product approach.
Enhanced performance on extreme weather days, including the case of July 1st, ensures our reliability even under the toughest conditions.
If you need more details, feel free to ask @Combe, Claudine & @Mériot, Clément
 

Additionally, we have released several versions of our Python shared libraries (Icarus, Hermes) to maximize value across multiple use cases (like the deep learning technics).

 

A big thank you to the entire team for achieving these results despite significant external challenges.🚀🚀🚀

 

 

Bernard
